hero.eyebrow: Private beta · Belgium · France · Netherlands — launching September 2026
hero.title: Your drivers, your vehicles, your revenue. One tool, two sides.
hero.subtitle: A dashboard for you. An app for your drivers. No spreadsheet, no manual math.

notify.privacyNote: One email: the launch day. No spam, no sharing.

success.subtitle: We'll write you as soon as Cabs is ready.

toast.alreadySubscribed: This email is already on the list.

features.title: What Cabs does for your fleet
features.subtitle: A platform built for operators who are done with spreadsheets.

aggregation.title: Multi-platform aggregation
aggregation.description: Uber, Bolt, Heetch, TaxiVert and cash. One source of truth, refreshed daily.

commissions.title: Gross, net, commissions
commissions.description: Cabs anticipates each platform's commission and shows you gross revenue and real net per driver, per vehicle, per period.

time.title: No more manual math
time.description: No more Excel, no more files sent over WhatsApp. Your numbers are live in the interface, available any time.

structure.title: Custom structure
structure.description: 50/50, flat-rate, rental. Configure your compensation model and Cabs applies it to your reports.

drivers.title: Drivers and vehicles
drivers.description: Profiles, availability, documents, deals. Your whole team in one view.

exports.title: Accounting exports
exports.description: PDF and Excel ready for your accountant, detailed by platform and by driver.

showcase.title: One dashboard. Everything that matters.
showcase.subtitle: The three screens your operators use every day.
showcase.interactiveHint: Click in the sidebar to explore each screen.

mobileNotice.title: Best on a large screen
mobileNotice.body: The dashboard simulator is dense (sidebar, tables, charts) and is designed for desktop.
mobileNotice.fallback: You can explore it here, but the optimal experience is on 1024 px and up.

pains.title: Sound like your Sunday evening?
pains.subtitle: If even one of these hits home, Cabs is built for you.

excel.title: An 8-tab Excel that breaks on every paste
excel.description: One tab per driver, formulas that snap, no idea who edited what.

paper.title: Paper trip sheets in every car
paper.description: Smudged ink, scribbles, blurry photo at 11pm. You retype everything the next morning.

whatsapp.title: Receipt photos dumped on WhatsApp
whatsapp.description: 12 drivers, 60 photos a week, decoded on Sunday night with a coffee.

clockin.title: No proof a driver actually clocked in
clockin.description: He was supposed to start at 6am. He says he did. You have zero evidence.

location.title: "Where are you?" calls every 30 minutes
location.description: No live tracking, no idea who's on a ride, who's on break, who's home.

reconciliation.title: Manual Uber × Bolt × Heetch reconciliation
reconciliation.description: You find out 3 months later that Bolt's rate changed. Too late to recover.

roi.title: How much time will Cabs save you?
roi.subtitle: Drag the sliders to your fleet. Numbers update live.

metrics.hoursWeekHint: Per week, on vehicle + driver admin

roi.disclaimer: Conservative estimates based on practices observed in the Belgian taxi industry (0.5h/wk per vehicle, 1.5h/wk per driver, €18/mo in commission errors per driver). Varies based on your current setup.

methodology.intro: Multipliers adapted to the Belgian context of operators managing drivers across Uber, Bolt, and Heetch. Public sources:
methodology.kfw: SMEs spend up to 8.7% of their working time on bureaucracy.
methodology.sage: small business owners lose the equivalent of one month of work per year to paperwork.
methodology.nyag: $328M Uber/Lyft settlement for systematic payment errors (~$91/mo/driver across 100,000+ drivers).
methodology.frVtc: 71,300 active VTC drivers in France in 2024 (+27% vs 2023).
methodology.nlTaxi: 14,400 taxi businesses in the Netherlands in 2024, of which 11,400 are self-employed (+29% in 5 years).
methodology.note: Our multipliers (0.5h/vehicle, 1.5h/driver, €18/driver) stay below the publicly documented gaps. Refined as the private beta progresses.

roi.expansion: Belgium, France, and the Netherlands from September. Germany following soon.

driverApp.title: The app your drivers keep in their pocket
driverApp.subtitle: Cabs isn't just a back-office tool. Your drivers get their own iOS and Android app, connected to the dashboard in real time.

revenue.title: Live revenue
revenue.description: Today's gross, net after commissions, comparison with yesterday. The driver knows what they earn, ride by ride.

alerts.title: Operator alerts
alerts.description: Broadcast a message to the whole team or a subset. It lands as a push notification, not a text message lost in the noise.

demoStart.subtitle: Try the driver app in 30 seconds.
demoStart.note: No install. This is what Cabs will look like in September.

location.title: Allow "Cabs" to use your location?
location.body: Cabs needs your location to dispatch rides, track your shift and validate trips.

locationBlocked.title: Location required
locationBlocked.body: Without access to your location, the app cannot dispatch rides or start a shift.
locationBlocked.reasonRides: Real-time Uber, Bolt, Heetch ride dispatch
locationBlocked.reasonAtas: Trip validation: ATAS (BE) / VTC (FR) / TTO (NL) — legal requirement
locationBlocked.antiSpoofBody: Any app altering GPS data (mock location, spoofing) is automatically flagged. The account is suspended and the operator is alerted.
locationBlocked.note: Cabs does not store your location outside of your shift.

team.title: Three software engineers, one concrete problem
team.subtitle: Three software engineers based in Brussels building Cabs for fleets across Belgium, France and the Netherlands. After watching operators juggle five tools, we decided to build one that fits them.

kristian.name: Kristian Vasiaj
bio: Software engineer specialising in backend and mobile. Kristian builds the API that powers the platform and the iOS/Android driver app — the one your drivers use every day to start their shifts and track their revenue in real time.
He owns the bridge between the driver's phone and the server, and makes sure the connection stays solid even when the network doesn't.

ismael.name: Ismael Bouzrouti
bio: Backend software engineer and technical architect. Ismael designs the structure of Cabs services on Java Spring Boot, TimescaleDB for the GPS streams and real-time vehicle telemetry, and RabbitMQ for asynchronous communication between services.

He also wears the Quality Assurance hat. No release ships without his sign-off: tests, monitoring, performance, edge cases. His philosophy is simple: a bug in production costs ten times more than a well-written test.

nawfel.name: Nawfel Ajari
bio: Frontend software engineer, specialising in React. Nawfel builds every web interface your operators use day to day: real-time dashboard, weekly schedule, driver and vehicle management, accounting exports.

His obsession: everything must be readable in three seconds. A dispatcher who can't find an info in the dashboard is a dispatcher who goes back to Excel. He also spends time with beta operators to turn their feedback into product specs.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Quick answers to the questions we get the most before public launch.

when.a: The private beta is running with a handful of Antwerp operators. Public launch is scheduled for September 2026. Subscribe to the notification list to get pinged on launch day.

timing.a: We're taking the time to code with quality, aiming for zero bugs at launch. The private beta runs with a handful of Antwerp operators to validate every feature in real conditions. The goal is a product ready for international from day one, not an MVP we patch after the fact.

minimum.q: What's the minimum number of vehicles?
minimum.a: For the private beta we work with fleets of 3+ vehicles to gather enough operational signal. At public launch, Cabs will be open to all sizes, from independent driver to large operator.

regions.q: Does Cabs work in Wallonia and Flanders?
regions.a: Yes, Cabs covers all of Belgium. Regional STP licences (Brussels, Wallonia, Flanders) and local constraints (ATAS, ONSS, legal scheduling) are taken into account.

integrations.q: How does Cabs pull my Uber, Bolt and Heetch rides?
integrations.a: You first configure your platforms and their commissions in the dashboard. At the end of each ride, the driver simply marks which app was used. Cabs automatically computes commissions and real net. No direct access to your Uber, Bolt or Heetch accounts is required, so zero risk on the platform side.

drivers.q: Are my drivers required to use the app?
drivers.a: No, you decide what to set up. The app is not mandatory. However, for the dashboard and calculations to be complete, work data sharing (shift hours, rides, platform used) is necessary. No personal data outside work is collected.

migration.q: How long does it take to move from Excel to Cabs?
migration.a: Your Excel files are converted to CSV via our AI pipeline, then imported into the platform by our team. Migration usually takes 24 to 48 hours. You start on Cabs with your history already in place.

accounting.q: Can my accountant pull exports easily?
accounting.a: Yes. Each data type (revenue per platform, per driver, per vehicle, period, VAT, commissions) ships with dedicated CSV and PDF exports, compatible with major Belgian accounting tools: Yuki, Octopus, BOB, Winbooks.

hosting.q: Where are my data and my drivers' data stored?
hosting.a: In a secure database, with end-to-end hashing on every sensitive field and encryption both in transit and at rest. Full GDPR compliance: right of access, modification and deletion are respected.

portability.q: If I leave Cabs, do I get my data back?
portability.a: Yes, no strings attached. You can export your full data (drivers, vehicles, rides, revenue, accounting) in CSV and PDF at any time. No retention games, your data is yours.

pricing.a: Final pricing will be published at launch. Founding operators (the first to join the beta) get a substantial lifetime discount as a thank you for their trust.

success.title: Message received!
success.subtitle: One of the three founders will write you personally within 48 business hours. Don't forget to check your spam folder.

terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.intro: Cabs is currently in private beta with Antwerp-based taxi operators. The operating company is being incorporated in Belgium. Full details (legal form, BCE number, registered office, VAT) will be published at public launch, scheduled for September 2026.

scope.title: Scope
scope.body: These terms govern the use of the joincabs.com showcase website. No paid service is offered at this stage. The only action available is subscribing to the launch notification list.

waitlist.title: Notification list
waitlist.body: By entering your email in the "Notify me" form, you accept to receive a single email on the day of public launch. No other communication will be sent. You can request deletion of your email at any time by writing to contact@joincabs.com.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Emails sent via the contact form are handled manually by the founding team. We reply within 48 business hours.

liability.title: Liability
liability.body: The information on this site is descriptive and non-contractual. Features, supported platforms and pricing may evolve before public launch.

law.title: Governing law
law.body: These terms are governed by Belgian law. Any dispute falls under the exclusive jurisdiction of the Brussels courts.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy describes how Cabs processes your personal data on this showcase website, in compliance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR) and the Belgian law of 30 July 2018.

controller.title: Data controller
controller.body: The co-founders (Kristian Vasiaj, Ismael Bouzrouti, Nawfel Ajari) act jointly as data controllers until the company is incorporated. Contact: contact@joincabs.com.

data.title: Data collected
data.body: Notification list: your email address and language preference. Contact form: name, email, company (optional), message. No other data is collected from this site.

purpose.title: Purposes
purpose.body: Notification list: sending you a single email on public launch day. Contact form: answering your request. No advertising, no profiling, no commercial sharing.

basis.title: Legal basis
basis.body: Explicit consent (article 6.1.a GDPR) for the notification list. Legitimate interest (article 6.1.f GDPR) for handling contact requests.

retention.title: Retention
retention.body: Notification list: up to 30 days after the launch email, then deletion. Contact messages: 12 months after the last interaction.

rights.title: Your rights
rights.body: You have the right to access, rectify, erase, port and object to the processing of your data. To exercise these rights, write to contact@joincabs.com. You can also file a complaint with the Belgian Data Protection Authority (www.dataprotectionauthority.be).

hosting.title: Hosting and processors
hosting.body: The site is hosted in the European Union. Notification list emails are stored on European servers. No transfer outside the EU is planned.

cookies.title: Cookies and trackers
cookies.body: This site uses no analytics cookies or advertising trackers. Only the browser's local storage is used to remember your language and theme (light/dark). No data is transmitted to any third party.

footer.preIncorporationNotice: Company under incorporation. Full details (BCE, registered office) published at public launch.
